channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: RuneScape Reduces Visual Clutter in Combat, Adds Better Targeting, and Double XP Returns This Week, With Tweaks
item.content:encoded:
The latest stop on RuneScape’s Road to Restoration has to do with reducing visual clutter, particularly during combat. Double XP is also returning this week, and there’s a new free gift bundle to claim.

With the Road to Restoration, Jagex aims to improve Gielinor and the overall RuneScape experience and they've been moving at a rapid pace to make these improvements since they were announced just a couple of months ago. The latest part of the ongoing project is reworking some combat related visuals to be less cluttered and to be more intuitive and helpful.

The team, in recent years, moved towards using squares, which are in line with the tile-based movement. Now, they are adding walk markers and target reticules. In the update notes (and in the header image here), you can see this in action with the target reticule around the boss. Its size is telling, and this will help you target and decide how to strategize and what you should be using. These will scale with target size. 

Walk markers are also available for additional clarity  and guidance.

If you don't want one or both of them, you can toggle them on or off in the Settings menu. 

The team also modifies a number of visual effects to make them less obtrusive and there are a list of examples in the patch notes so you can get an idea of the before and after. 

Double XP is returning on May 1st, with several changes based on prior Road to Restoration updates. What hasn't changed is you still get 48 hours of Double XP to use when you want over the 10-day period. What has changed is no more daily login incentives (after DailyScape changes). This will also be the final time that you can earn Double XP rokens and use them at the store. 

If you have any tokens you should spend them at the Double XP Token Store,  because this event will be the last time the shop is available and any leftover tokens you have after the event ends will be converted into GP. More details on this event are in the patch notes, including outfit availability, and more that will shift based on their “integrity” efforts.

There is a free gift bundle in the Marketplace to claim, now that Solomon has closed shop: Solomon's Loyalty Point Bundle.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/runescape-reduces-visual-clutter-in-combat-adds-better-targeting-and-double-xp-returns-this-week-with-tweaks-2000137908
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
